# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3.8'

services:
  # Elasticsearch - Log Storage and Search Engine
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=es-node-01
      - cluster.name=elk-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - bootstrap.memory_lock=true
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
      - xpack.license.self_generated.type=basic
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.threshold_enabled=true
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.low=85%
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.high=90%
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.flood_stage=95%
      - indices.lifecycle.poll_interval=1m
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
      nofile:
        soft: 65536
        hard: 65536
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - ./elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:ro
      - ./elasticsearch/config/ilm-policy.json:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/ilm-policy.json:ro
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
      - "9300:9300"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q '\"status\":\"green\"\\|\"status\":\"yellow\"'"
        ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
          cpus: '2.0'
        reservations:
          memory: 1g
          cpus: '0.5'

  # Logstash - Log Collection and Parsing Pipeline
  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.12.0
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - "LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD=${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - xpack.monitoring.enabled=true
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username=elastic
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    volumes:
      - ./logstash/config/logstash.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/logstash.yml:ro
      - ./logstash/config/pipelines.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/pipelines.yml:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline/main.conf:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/main.conf:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline/syslog.conf:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/syslog.conf:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline/nginx.conf:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline/application.conf:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/application.conf:ro
      - ./logstash/patterns:/usr/share/logstash/patterns:ro
      - logstash_data:/usr/share/logstash/data
      - logstash_sincedb:/usr/share/logstash/sincedb
    ports:
      - "5044:5044"       # Beats input
      - "5000:5000/tcp"   # TCP input (generic logs)
      - "5000:5000/udp"   # UDP input (generic logs)
      - "5514:5514/tcp"   # Syslog TCP
      - "5514:5514/udp"   # Syslog UDP
      - "9600:9600"       # Logstash monitoring API
      - "8080:8080"       # HTTP input for webhooks/APIs
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "curl -s http://localhost:9600/_node/stats | grep -q '\"status\":\"green\"' || curl -s http://localhost:9600 | grep -q 'logstash'"
        ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 90s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
          cpus: '1.5'
        reservations:
          memory: 512m
          cpus: '0.25'

  # Kibana - Log Visualization and Dashboard UI
  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.12.0
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME=kibana_system
      - ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - ELASTICSEARCH_SSL_VERIFICATIONMODE=none
      - SERVER_NAME=kibana
      - SERVER_HOST=0.0.0.0
      - SERVER_PORT=5601
      - SERVER_BASEPATH=${KIBANA_BASE_PATH:-}
      - XPACK_SECURITY_ENABLED=true
      - XPACK_ENCRYPTEDSAVEDOBJECTS_ENCRYPTIONKEY=${KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY:-a]V67MfDNB0Oy{+L1dpJPOW:pa5snq3R}
      - XPACK_REPORTING_ENCRYPTIONKEY=${KIBANA_REPORTING_KEY:-FgJOCq5bL0mz9gt8xO3*mN+XQfGJNq3R}
      - XPACK_SECURITY_ENCRYPTIONKEY=${KIBANA_SECURITY_KEY:-Q7FJBCw4%5Yxrt8nO3*kN+UQfPJN93eR}
      - LOGGING_ROOT_LEVEL=info
      - MONITORING_UI_CONTAINER_ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLED=true
    volumes:
      - ./kibana/config/kibana.yml:/usr/share/kibana/config/kibana.yml:ro
      - kibana_data:/usr/share/kibana/data
    ports:
      - "5601:5601"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} http://localhost:5601/api/status | grep -q 'available'"
        ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 120s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
          cpus: '1.0'
        reservations:
          memory: 512m
          cpus: '0.25'

  # Filebeat - Lightweight Log Shipper (optional agent)
  filebeat:
    image: docker.elastic.co/beats/filebeat:8.12.0
    container_name: filebeat
    user: root
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    volumes:
      - ./filebeat/config/filebeat.yml:/usr/share/filebeat/filebeat.yml:ro
      - filebeat_data:/usr/share/filebeat/data
      - /var/log:/var/log/host:ro
      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
      logstash:
        condition: service_healthy
    command: ["filebeat", "-e", "--strict.perms=false"]
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "filebeat test config || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 256m
          cpus: '0.5'
        reservations:
          memory: 128m
          cpus: '0.1'

  # Setup service - Initializes passwords and index templates
  setup:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elk-setup
    volumes:
      - ./setup/entrypoint.sh:/entrypoint.sh:ro
      - ./setup/ilm-policy.json:/ilm-policy.json:ro
      - ./setup/index-template.json:/index-template.json:ro
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD=${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "/entrypoint.sh"]
    restart: "no"

  # Curator - Index Lifecycle Management (cleanup old indices)
  curator:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: curator
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - RETENTION_DAYS=${RETENTION_DAYS:-30}
    volumes:
      - ./curator/cleanup.sh:/cleanup.sh:ro
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    entrypoint: >
      /bin/bash -c '
        while true; do
          echo "[Curator] Running index cleanup at $$(date)";
          /bin/bash /cleanup.sh;
          echo "[Curator] Next run in 24 hours";
          sleep 86400;
        done
      '
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128m
          cpus: '0.25'

  # Nginx reverse proxy for Kibana (optional - production hardening)
  nginx:
    image: nginx:1.25-alpine
    container_name: elk-nginx
    volumes:
      - ./nginx/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./nginx/conf.d:/etc/nginx/conf.d:ro
      - ./nginx/ssl:/etc/nginx/ssl:ro
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      kibana:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:80/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128m
          cpus: '0.25'

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
    driver: local
    driver_opts:
      type: none
      device: ${ES_DATA_PATH:-./data/elasticsearch}
      o: bind
  logstash_data:
    driver: local
  logstash_sincedb:
    driver: local
  kibana_data:
    driver: local
  filebeat_data:
    driver: local

networks:
  elk-network:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: 172.28.0.0/16

# ===========================================================================
# ADDITIONAL CONFIGURATION FILES (create these in the project directory)
# ===========================================================================
#
# --- .env ---
# ELASTIC_PASSWORD=your_secure_elastic_password
# KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD=your_secure_kibana_password
# LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD=your_secure_logstash_password
# KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=32_char_random_string_here_12345
# KIBANA_REPORTING_KEY=32_char_random_string_here_67890
# KIBANA_SECURITY_KEY=32_char_random_string_here_abcde
# RETENTION_DAYS=30
# ES_DATA_PATH=./data/elasticsearch
# KIBANA_BASE_PATH=
#
# --- elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml ---
# cluster.name: elk-cluster
# node.name: es-node-01
# network.host: 0.0.0.0
# http.port: 9200
# path.data: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
# path.logs: /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs
#
# --- logstash/config/logstash.yml ---
# http.host: "0.0.0.0"
# path.config: /usr/share/logstash/pipeline
# pipeline.workers: 2
# pipeline.batch.size: 125
# pipeline.batch.delay: 50
# queue.type: persisted
# queue.max_bytes: 1gb
# dead_letter_queue.enable: true
# log.level: info
#
# --- logstash/config/pipelines.yml ---
# - pipeline.id: main
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/main.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 2
# - pipeline.id: syslog
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/syslog.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 1
# - pipeline.id: nginx
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/nginx.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 1
# - pipeline.id: application
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/application.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 1
#
# --- logstash/pipeline/main.conf ---
# input {
#   beats {
#     port => 5044
#     ssl => false
#   }
#   tcp {
#     port => 5000
#     codec => json_lines
#     tags => ["tcp_input"]
#   }
#   udp {
#     port => 5000
#     codec => json
#     tags => ["udp_input"]
#   }
#   http {
#     port => 8080
#     codec => json
#     tags => ["http_input"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   # Add timestamp processing
#   if ![timestamp] {
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "timestamp" => "%{@timestamp}" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse JSON message if present
#   if [message] =~ /^\{/ {
#     json {
#       source => "message"
#       target => "parsed_json"
#       skip_on_invalid_json => true
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Add GeoIP for IP fields
#   if [source_ip] {
#     geoip {
#       source => "source_ip"
#       target => "geoip"
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Add environment tags
#   mutate {
#     add_field => {
#       "[@metadata][env]" => "${ENVIRONMENT:production}"
#       "pipeline" => "main"
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Remove unnecessary fields
#   mutate {
#     remove_field => ["host", "@version"]
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   elasticsearch {
#     hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#     user => "elastic"
#     password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#     index => "logs-generic-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     manage_template => true
#     template_overwrite => true
#   }
#
#   # Debug output (disable in production)
#   # stdout { codec => rubydebug }
# }
#
# --- logstash/pipeline/syslog.conf ---
# input {
#   syslog {
#     port => 5514
#     type => "syslog"
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   if [type] == "syslog" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => [
#           "%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{DATA:syslog_program}(?:\[%{POSINT:syslog_pid}\])?: %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}",
#           "%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}"
#         ]
#       }
#       overwrite => ["message"]
#     }
#
#     date {
#       match => ["syslog_timestamp", "MMM  d HH:mm:ss", "MMM dd HH:mm:ss"]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#
#     # Classify severity
#     if [severity] {
#       translate {
#         field => "severity"
#         destination => "severity_label"
#         dictionary => {
#           "0" => "Emergency"
#           "1" => "Alert"
#           "2" => "Critical"
#           "3" => "Error"
#           "4" => "Warning"
#           "5" => "Notice"
#           "6" => "Informational"
#           "7" => "Debug"
#         }
#         fallback => "Unknown"
#       }
#     }
#
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "pipeline" => "syslog" }
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if [type] == "syslog" {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "elastic"
#       password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "logs-syslog-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# --- logstash/pipeline/nginx.conf ---
# input {
#   beats {
#     port => 5045
#     tags => ["nginx"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   if "nginx" in [tags] {
#     # Parse NGINX access logs
#     if [fileset][name] == "access" or [log][file][path] =~ "access" {
#       grok {
#         match => {
#           "message" => [
#             "%{IPORHOST:remote_addr} - %{DATA:remote_user} \[%{HTTPDATE:time_local}\] \"%{WORD:request_method} %{DATA:request_uri} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}\" %{INT:status:int} %{NUMBER:body_bytes_sent:int} \"%{DATA:http_referer}\" \"%{DATA:http_user_agent}\" \"%{DATA:http_x_forwarded_for}\" rt=%{NUMBER:request_time:float} urt=%{NUMBER:upstream_response_time:float}",
#             "%{IPORHOST:remote_addr} - %{DATA:remote_user} \[%{HTTPDATE:time_local}\] \"%{WORD:request_method} %{DATA:request_uri} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}\" %{INT:status:int} %{NUMBER:body_bytes_sent:int} \"%{DATA:http_referer}\" \"%{DATA:http_user_agent}\"",
#             "%{COMBINEDAPACHELOG}"
#           ]
#         }
#       }
#
#       date {
#         match => ["time_local", "dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss Z"]
#         target => "@timestamp"
#       }
#
#       # Categorize HTTP status codes
#       if [status] {
#         if [status] >= 200 and [status] < 300 {
#           mutate { add_field => { "status_category" => "success" } }
#         } else if [status] >= 300 and [status] < 400 {
#           mutate { add_field => { "status_category" => "redirect" } }
#         } else if [status] >= 400 and [status] < 500 {
#           mutate { add_field => { "status_category" => "client_error" } }
#         } else if [status] >= 500 {
#           mutate { add_field => { "status_category" => "server_error" } }
#         }
#       }
#
#       # Parse User Agent
#       useragent {
#         source => "http_user_agent"
#         target => "user_agent"
#       }
#
#       # GeoIP lookup
#       if [remote_addr] and [remote_addr] !~ /^(127\.|10\.|192\.168\.|172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[01])\.)/ {
#         geoip {
#           source => "remote_addr"
#           target => "geoip"
#           add_tag => ["geoip"]
#         }
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Parse NGINX error logs
#     if [fileset][name] == "error" or [log][file][path] =~ "error" {
#       grok {
#         match => {
#           "message" => "%{DATA:time} \[%{DATA:log_level}\] %{NUMBER:pid}#%{NUMBER:tid}: (\*%{NUMBER:connection_id} )?%{GREEDYDATA:error_message}"
#         }
#       }
#     }
#
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "pipeline" => "nginx" }
#       remove_field => ["time_local"]
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if "nginx" in [tags] {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "elastic"
#       password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "logs-nginx-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# --- logstash/pipeline/application.conf ---
# input {
#   tcp {
#     port => 5001
#     codec => json_lines
#     tags => ["application"]
#   }
#   http {
#     port => 8081
#     codec => json
#     tags => ["application"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   if "application" in [tags] {
#     # Parse log level
#     if [level] {
#       mutate {
#         uppercase => ["level"]
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Parse stack traces (Java-style)
#     if [message] =~ /Exception|Error|Traceback/ {
#       mutate {
#         add_tag => ["exception"]
#       }
#       # Multiline codec alternative: aggregate stack traces
#       grok {
#         match => {
#           "message" => "(?<exception_class>[a-zA-Z0-9_.]+Exception|[a-zA-Z0-9_.]+Error): %{GREEDYDATA:exception_message}"
#         }
#         tag_on_failure => []
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Parse structured application logs
#     if [message] =~ /^\{/ {
#       json {
#         source => "message"
#         target => "app_data"
#         skip_on_invalid_json => true
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Extract request duration if present
#     if [duration] {
#       mutate {
#         convert => { "duration" => "float" }
#       }
#       # Flag slow requests
#       if [duration] > 5000 {
#         mutate {
#           add_tag => ["slow_request"]
#         }
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Fingerprint for deduplication
#     fingerprint {
#       source => ["message", "@timestamp", "service"]
#       target => "[@metadata][fingerprint]"
#       method => "SHA256"
#     }
#
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "pipeline" => "application" }
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if "application" in [tags] {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "elastic"
#       password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "logs-app-%{[service]:unknown}-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#       document_id => "%{[@metadata][fingerprint]}"
#     }
#
#     # Send critical errors to a dedicated index
#     if [level] == "ERROR" or [level] == "FATAL" or "exception" in [tags] {
#       elasticsearch {
#         hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#         user => "elastic"
#         password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#         index => "logs-errors-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#       }
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# --- filebeat/config/filebeat.yml ---
# filebeat.inputs:
#   - type: container
#     paths:
#       - '/var/lib/docker/containers/*/*.log'
#     processors:
#       - add_docker_metadata:
#           host: "unix:///var/run/docker.sock"
#       - decode_json_fields:
#           fields: ["message"]
#           target: ""
#           overwrite_keys: true
#
#   - type: log
#     enabled: true
#     paths:
#       - /var/log/host/*.log
#       - /var/log/host/syslog
#     tags: ["system"]
#
# filebeat.autodiscover:
#   providers:
#     - type: docker
#       hints.enabled: true
#       hints.default_config:
#         type: container
#         paths:
#           - /var/lib/docker/containers/${data.container.id}/*.log
#
# output.logstash:
#   hosts: ["logstash:5044"]
#   bulk_max_size: 1024
#   worker: 2
#
# processors:
#   - add_host_metadata: ~
#   - add_cloud_metadata: ~
#   - add_docker_metadata: ~
#
# monitoring:
#   enabled: true
#   elasticsearch:
#     hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#     username: elastic
#     password: ${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}
#
# logging.level: info
# logging.to_files: true
# logging.files:
#   path: /usr/share/filebeat/logs
#   name: filebeat
#   keepfiles: 7
#   permissions: 0644
#
# --- kibana/config/kibana.yml ---
# server.host: "0.0.0.0"
# server.port: 5601
# server.name: "kibana"
# elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
# elasticsearch.username: "kibana_system"
# elasticsearch.password: "${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD}"
# elasticsearch.requestTimeout: 60000
# monitoring.ui.container.elasticsearch.enabled: true
# telemetry.optIn: false
# newsfeed.enabled: false
# logging:
#   appenders:
#     file:
#       type: file
#       fileName: /usr/share/kibana/logs/kibana.log
#       layout:
#         type: json
#   root:
#     appenders: [default, file]
#     level: info
#
# --- nginx/conf.d/default.conf ---
# upstream kibana {
#     server kibana:5601;
# }
#
# server {
#     listen 80;
#     server_name _;
#
#     location /health {
#         access_log off;
#         return 200 "healthy\n";
#         add_header Content-Type text/plain;
#     }
#
#     location / {
#         proxy_pass http://kibana;
#         proxy_http_version 1.1;
#         proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
#         proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
#         proxy_set_header Host $host;
#         proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
#         proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
#         proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
#         proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
#         proxy_read_timeout 90;
#         proxy_buffering off;
#     }
# }
#
# --- nginx/nginx.conf ---
# worker_processes auto;
# error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log warn;
# pid /var/run/nginx.pid;
#
# events {
#     worker_connections 1024;
# }
#
# http {
#     include /etc/nginx/mime.types;
#     default_type application/octet-stream;
#     log_format main '$remote_addr - $remote_user [$time_local] "$request" '
#                     '$status $body_bytes_sent "$http_referer" '
#                     '"$http_user_agent" "$http_x_forwarded_for"';
#     access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log main;
#     sendfile on;
#     keepalive_timeout 65;
#     client_max_body_size 50m;
#     include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf;
# }
#
# --- setup/entrypoint.sh ---
# #!/bin/bash
# set -euo pipefail
#
# echo "Waiting for Elasticsearch to be ready..."
# until curl -s -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" http://elasticsearch:9200 | grep -q "cluster_name"; do
#     sleep 5
# done
# echo "Elasticsearch is ready!"
#
# # Set kibana_system password
# echo "Setting kibana_system password..."
# curl -s -X POST -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/kibana_system/_password \
#   -d "{\"password\":\"${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD}\"}"
#
# # Set logstash_internal password
# echo "Setting logstash_internal user..."
# curl -s -X POST -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/logstash_internal/_password \
#   -d "{\"password\":\"${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD}\"}" || true
#
# # Create ILM policy
# echo "Creating ILM policy..."
# curl -s -X PUT -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_ilm/policy/logs-policy \
#   -d '{
#     "policy": {
#       "phases": {
#         "hot": {
#           "actions": {
#             "rollover": {
#               "max_primary_shard_size": "50gb",
#               "max_age": "1d"
#             }
#           }
#         },
#         "warm": {
#           "min_age": "7d",
#           "actions": {
#             "shrink": { "number_of_shards": 1 },
#             "forcemerge": { "max_num_segments": 1 }
#           }
#         },
#         "delete": {
#           "min_age": "30d",
#           "actions": { "delete": {} }
#         }
#       }
#     }
#   }'
#
# # Create index templates
# echo "Creating index templates..."
# for pattern in generic syslog nginx app errors; do
#   curl -s -X PUT -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
#     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#     http://elasticsearch:9200/_index_template/logs-${pattern}-template \
#     -d "{
#       \"index_patterns\": [\"logs-${pattern}-*\"],
#       \"template\": {
#         \"settings\": {
#           \"number_of_shards\": 1,
#           \"number_of_replicas\": 0,
#           \"index.lifecycle.name\": \"logs-policy\"
#         }
#       }
#     }"
# done
#
# echo "Setup completed successfully!"
#
# --- curator/cleanup.sh ---
# #!/bin/bash
# # Delete indices older than RETENTION_DAYS
# RETENTION_DAYS=${RETENTION_DAYS:-30}
# DATE=$(date -d "${RETENTION_DAYS} days ago" +%Y.%m.%d 2>/dev/null || date -v-${RETENTION_DAYS}d +%Y.%m.%d)
#
# echo "Cleaning up indices older than ${RETENTION_DAYS} days (before ${DATE})"
#
# curl -s -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
#   "http://elasticsearch:9200/_cat/indices/logs-*?h=index&s=index" | \
#   while read index; do
#     index_date=$(echo "$index" | grep -oP '\d{4}\.\d{2}\.\d{2}$' || true)
#     if [ -n "$index_date" ] && [ "$index_date" \< "$DATE" ]; then
#       echo "Deleting old index: $index"
#       curl -s -X DELETE -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
#         "http://elasticsearch:9200/${index}"
#     fi
#   done
#
# echo "Cleanup complete"
# ===========================================================================
